calendar.matters:
PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF DEATH
Off Calendar Feb-03-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom: Asher Anderson, Esq., via Zoom, for and with Megan Johnson (Petitioner), via Zoom.

The Court stated that it had read this very sympathetic presentation. The Court and counsel confer regarding Probate Code 200. Mr. Anderson argued that Probate Code 200 would provide similar relief, that they are seeking a certified copy of a death certificate which is less problematic, that in the past when he tried to proceed with a court order that there were problems, and that a certificate of death is better. The Court stated that it will look at the health and safety application, that it is not one hundred percent sure that there was a death because a body was not recovered, and that it needs to make that finding. The Court addressed Probate Code 200 stating that it would be supported by a declaration that the petitioning party would present which represents that the petitioning party is not aware of anyone that would come forward and object, that this Court does not know if Mr. Tran had parents or siblings, that under Probate Code 200 notice is given, that this Court is sympathetic, wants to help, but needs to have this discussion. Ms. Johnson stated that she is certain that there are no such other people that would come forward.

At the conclusion of argument/discussions, the Court took the petition to establish fact of death under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.
